dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 7629 IH: Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act and Dingell-Johnson Sport Fish Restoration Act District of Columbia Equality Act of 2026
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Ms. Norton introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Natural Resources

form.official-title: To amend the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act and Dingell-Johnson Sport Fish Restoration Act to treat the District of Columbia as a State for the purposes of such Acts, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act and Dingell-Johnson Sport Fish Restoration Act District of Columbia Equality Act of 2026.

section.header: Treatment of District of Columbia under Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act and Dingell-Johnson Sport Fish Restoration Act

subsection.text: The Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act (16 U.S.C. 669 et seq.) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by redesignating paragraphs (6) through (11) as paragraphs (7) through (12), respectively; and
by inserting after paragraph (5) the following:

paragraph.text: the term State means each of the several States and the District of Columbia;

subparagraph.text: in subsection (d)(1)(A), by striking District of Columbia and to the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, each and inserting Commonwealth of Puerto Rico,; and
in subsection (e)(5), by striking the District of Columbia,.

subsection.text: The Dingell-Johnson Sport Fish Restoration Act (16 U.S.C. 777 et seq.) is amended—

subparagraph.text: by striking and at the end of paragraph (2);
by striking the period at the end of paragraph (3) and inserting ; and; and
by adding at the end the following:

paragraph.text: the term State means each of the several States and the District of Columbia.
in section 4(c)(1) (16 U.S.C. 777c(c)(1)), by striking several; and

clause.text: by striking the Mayor of the District of Columbia,;
by striking the District of Columbia,; and
by striking for the District of Columbia one-third of 1 per centum,; and

subparagraph.text: in the second sentence, by striking the District of Columbia,.
